item.title:
Last month, we published our look into what 81,000 people told us they want from AI.
In new research, we’ve investigated the economic hopes and worries referenced in their responses.
Read more: http
item.description:
Last month, we published our look into what 81,000 people told us they want from AI.
In new research, we’ve investigated the economic hopes and worries referenced in their responses.
Read more: https://t.co/PtKjamGmfg

item.title:
We're launching the Anthropic STEM Fellows Program.
AI will accelerate progress in science and engineering. We're looking for experts across these fields to work alongside our research teams on spec
item.description:
We're launching the Anthropic STEM Fellows Program.
AI will accelerate progress in science and engineering. We're looking for experts across these fields to work alongside our research teams on specific projects over a few months.
Learn more and apply: https://t.co/MoF60j53pX

item.title: We're expanding our collaboration with Amazon to secure up to 5 gigawatts of compute for training and deploying Claude. Capacity begins coming online this quarter, with nearly 1 gigawatt expected by t
item.description: We're expanding our collaboration with Amazon to secure up to 5 gigawatts of compute for training and deploying Claude. Capacity begins coming online this quarter, with nearly 1 gigawatt expected by the end of 2026.

item.title:
RT @claudeai: Introducing Claude Opus 4.7, our most capable Opus model yet.
It handles long-running tasks with more rigor, follows instruc…
item.description:
RT @claudeai: Introducing Claude Opus 4.7, our most capable Opus model yet.
It handles long-running tasks with more rigor, follows instruc…

item.title:
Research we co-authored on subliminal learning—how LLMs can pass on traits like preferences or misalignment through hidden signals in data—was published today in @Nature.
Read the paper: https://t.c
item.description:
Research we co-authored on subliminal learning—how LLMs can pass on traits like preferences or misalignment through hidden signals in data—was published today in @Nature.
Read the paper: https://t.co/b1BYwcW9dH

item.title:
New Anthropic Fellows research: developing an Automated Alignment Researcher.
We ran an experiment to learn whether Claude Opus 4.6 could accelerate research on a key alignment problem: using a weak
item.description:
New Anthropic Fellows research: developing an Automated Alignment Researcher.
We ran an experiment to learn whether Claude Opus 4.6 could accelerate research on a key alignment problem: using a weak AI model to supervise the training of a stronger one.
https://t.co/OAxCjOiWTm

item.title:
Our Long-Term Benefit Trust has appointed Vas Narasimhan to Anthropic's Board of Directors.
Vas brings more than two decades of experience in medicine and global health, including as CEO of Novartis.
item.description:
Our Long-Term Benefit Trust has appointed Vas Narasimhan to Anthropic's Board of Directors.
Vas brings more than two decades of experience in medicine and global health, including as CEO of Novartis.
Read more: https://t.co/DSvtZUTone

item.title:
New on the Engineering Blog:
Building Managed Agents—our hosted service for long-running agents—meant solving an old problem in computing: how to design a system for “programs as yet unthought of.”
item.description:
New on the Engineering Blog:
Building Managed Agents—our hosted service for long-running agents—meant solving an old problem in computing: how to design a system for “programs as yet unthought of.”
Read more: https://t.co/YYaEub2QGV

item.title:
Introducing Project Glasswing: an urgent initiative to help secure the world’s most critical software.
It’s powered by our newest frontier model, Claude Mythos Preview, which can find software vulner
item.description:
Introducing Project Glasswing: an urgent initiative to help secure the world’s most critical software.
It’s powered by our newest frontier model, Claude Mythos Preview, which can find software vulnerabilities better than all but the most skilled humans. https://t.co/NQ7IfEtYk7

item.title: We've signed an agreement with Google and Broadcom for multiple gigawatts of next-generation TPU capacity, coming online starting in 2027, to train and serve frontier Claude models.
item.description: We've signed an agreement with Google and Broadcom for multiple gigawatts of next-generation TPU capacity, coming online starting in 2027, to train and serve frontier Claude models.
